text_excerpt: --> About | Sarah Owen MP top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. ABOUT ME I am the Labour Member of Parliament for Luton North, a constituency covering the neighbourhoods of Limbury, Lewsey, Leagrave, Sundon Park, Marsh Farm, Saints, Challney, Barnfield, Bushmead, Bramingham and Stopsley. ​ I was first elected in the General Election of 2019, and re-elected in 2024 to continue working hard as Luton North's MP. ​ I started my career working for the NHS, and it was working as a Healthcare Assistant and carer that gave me a strong appreciation for the dedication of our NHS staff. Throughout my career I’ve campaigned on getting a better deal for workers, on healthcare inequalities, human rights, antisocial behaviour, accessibility and road safety. I am proud to represent Luton North and champion our thriving and diverse communities. ​ In Parliament, I am also Chair of the Women and Equalities Committee , working to scrutinise the government in its policies that affect marginalised communities across the country. ​ ​ Luton is my home - I love it here and I am proud to represent this part of our diverse and vibrant town in Parliament. WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH bluesky Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN © 2025 Office of Sarah Owen MP bottom of page
